title: torch-npu runtime requires 8 undeclared system dependencies
problem_description: torch-npu 2.5.1 depends on decorator, attrs, psutil, absl-py, cloudpickle, ml-dtypes, scipy, and tornado at runtime, but none are declared in its pip metadata. These packages are pulled in transitively when PyYAML and other dependencies are resolved, but may still be missing in minimal venvs. Missing any of these causes import failures or runtime errors when torch-npu features are accessed.
rough_fix_approach: After installing torch-npu, run a pre-flight import validation to catch missing dependencies early: python -c 'import torch_npu; import torch_npu.npu'. If this fails, install the missing packages from the NPU stack: pip install decorator attrs psutil absl-py cloudpickle ml-dtypes scipy tornado.
artifact_evidence: validated/phase_fix_dependency_canonical.json: summary lists 'installed 8 missing NPU stack deps (decorator, attrs, psutil, absl-py, cloudpickle, ml-dtypes, scipy, tornado)'
validated/phase_5_validation_canonical.json: loop_history iteration 1 completed after dependency fixes
reasoning: Multiple NPU stack dependencies are undeclared, but this is partially mitigated if PyYAML is installed first (it pulls in some transitives). The remaining packages may vary by CANN version. A pre-flight import check is more robust than hardcoding a package list. Verified on CANN 8.1.RC1.
